etymology.description: 鼎(鼎) (dǐng, "cauldron") + 廾(廾) (gǒng, "two hands") = preparing food
etymology.interpretation: The character 具(jù, "tool; implement") combines 鼎(鼎) (dǐng, "cauldron") and 廾(廾) (gǒng, "two hands") to depict the preparation of food, providing the intuitive connection to implements or tools used for preparation.

examples.chinese: 这个工具对修理很有帮助(這個工具對修理很有幫助)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège gōngjù duì xiūlǐ hěn yǒu bāngzhù.
examples.english: This tool is very helpful for repairs.

examples.chinese: 他们在做实验时需要很多设备和工具(他們在做實驗時需要很多設備和工具)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài zuò shíyàn shí xūyào hěn duō shèbèi hé gōngjù.
examples.english: They need a lot of equipment and tools for conducting experiments.

examples.chinese: 我们需要一些特殊的工具来完成这个项目(我們需要一些特殊的工具來完成這個項目)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào yīxiē tèshū de gōngjù lái wánchéng zhège xiàngmù.
examples.english: We need some special tools to complete this project.
